method_atom: a measurement scheme that recovers the value of a phase-space quasiprobability distribution at a point by combining two objects and counting discrete detection events, exploiting the fact that the overlap of two such distributions equals a measurable coincidence/counting statistic; in an appropriate limit this yields the distribution directly without any inverse-reconstruction (tomographic) algorithm, with a correction analysis for imperfect (sub-unit-efficiency) detectors
object_atom: the quantum state of a single-mode light beam, characterized by its Wigner function / phase-space quasiprobability distribution, measured via photon counting in an optical setup that mixes two single-mode fields
brief_reason: what carries across is the overlap-equals-counting-statistic estimation principle for a phase-space distribution obtained without tomographic inversion; what is domain-specific is the single-mode quantized light field and its photodetection realization.
